steps.title: Separate early and later styles
steps.content: Calle Liceo is described as an earlier Guadalajara project showing traditional Mediterranean and Mexican aesthetics. After Barragán's early-1930s travel, his later Avenida Parque México houses used functionalist and modernist principles, including unadorned geometric forms.

steps.title: Use the stated contrast
steps.content: Choice A wrongly calls Calle Liceo transitional. Choice B assigns later international influences to the earlier house. Choice D claims a lifelong influence the text does not support. The house reflects a traditional approach Barragán later moved away from. The answer is C.
